name: Amazonia

notes: All 20 frozen rows have exact current formulation matches; 14 additional current formulations were absent from the frozen dinner-only catalog.

The asserted parser validates 23 dinner CMS rows, eight Anticuchería rows, 46 cold/hot display rows, 12 Sour Hour rows, three desserts, four zero-proof rows, the nonalcoholic sparkling product, and espresso before consolidation.
Twenty-four current formulations have fixed restaurant-issued positive ingredient signals and ten remain accurately unavailable.
Seven frozen rows were underreported; no frozen positive claim was overreported against the current first-party wording.
Reviewed Webflow conditional visibility, section-parent association, cross-menu consolidation, dietary legend semantics, alcohol exclusions, and generated persistence.
Ten focused tests pass; target-only repair preserved every non-Amazonia record hash, and a second target-only run preserved Amazonia’s record hash.

findings.summary: The frozen 20-row dinner extraction omitted three current dinner formulations and every separately published Sour Hour food, dessert, coffee, and zero-proof formulation.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt 34 current formulations from all four food/nonalcoholic menu surfaces and consolidated 43 source presentations.

findings.summary: Seven frozen rows omitted current positive allergen signals: five anticucho options lost the parent soy-sauce marinade and two explicit octopus dishes lost shellfish.
findings.resolution: Associated each Anticuchería child with its restaurant-issued section description and mapped explicitly named octopus to the app shellfish category.

findings.summary: Amazonia’s c/d/e/g/s letters are absence or accommodation codes, with parenthesized letters indicating modifiability; they are not positive contains fields or a complete allergen matrix.
findings.resolution: Preserved codes in source presentations for auditability but derived positive allergens only from fixed ingredient wording; no negative or unsupported cross-contact claim was created.

repairs.summary: Replaced Amazonia’s partial dinner-only catalog with the asserted 34-formulation current first-party food and nonalcoholic catalog and corrected seven allergen underreports.

notes: The dinner legend defines c as celiac friendly (no cross contamination), d as dairy free, e as egg free, g as gluten friendly, s as soy free, v as vegetarian, ve as vegan, and parentheses as modifiable for allergies.

Amazonia’s current overview links Dinner, Drinks, Dessert, and Sour Hour as separate official menu surfaces; alcoholic beverage rows were excluded while published zero-proof beverages and espresso were retained.
